news.headline: SigmaTron International, Inc. announced Q3 fiscal results with earnings and revenues for quarter ended Jan 31, 2025.
news.summary: SigmaTron International, Inc., headquartered in Elk Grove Village, Illinois, released its financial results for the fiscal quarter concluding on January 31, 2025. The company, listed on the NASDAQ as SGMA, disclosed its revenues and earnings for this period.

news.headline: Trump ends remarks at the Justice Department.
FDA updates flu vaccine composition for 2025-2026 season, as announced on March 14, 2025 in Silver Spring, Maryland.
news.summary: In Silver Spring, Maryland on March 14, 2025, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration released a summary of recent updates. The FDA announced details regarding the flu vaccine composition for the upcoming 2025-2026 flu season. This information was posted on Thursday, providing insight into the vaccines to be used in the United States.

news.headline: Crypto tumbles post-hack, $1.4bn Bybit attack involves North Korea, SOL struggles, Sonic & Berachain surge. ETH reserve restored, safe site musk attacked. Bybit offers 10% reward for returned funds. Large amounts of hacked funds blocked, Safe beefs up security. Tracks of stolen BTC & SOL. Solana at lowest since Dec '23. Franklin Templeton files for SOL ETF. Binance unloads SOL via Wintermute. Berachain & Sonic see top weekly inflows. Ethena raises $100m for tradfi, ARB to invest 7.5k ETH. El Salvador to cease BTC purchases. Strategy eyes $2bn BTC buy, ARK spot trade broadens, Monad testnet at 10k TPS.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency prices experienced a decline following a significant sell-off in the macro market and a major hack. North Korea’s Lazarus group was identified as the culprit behind the $1.4 billion hack of Bybit. The hack was facilitated by a musking attack on the Safe website. Bybit managed to restore its ETH reserves after implementing loans and a buyback strategy. The platform offered a 10% reward for returned funds, with a substantial amount of hacked funds already blocked. In response to the hack, Safe is enhancing its security measures. The hacker now holds more ETH than the ETH Foundation. Additionally, 0xInfini fell victim to a $50 million hack. Despite calls for an Ethereum rollback, they were broadly dismissed. The SOLBTC pairing dropped to its lowest level since December 2023. Franklin Templeton filed for a SOL ETF, while Binance sold large blocks of SOL through Wintermute. Berachain and Sonic led weekly inflows, with BERA surpassing ARB and Base in Total Value Locked (TVL). Ethena secured $100 million for its traditional finance (tradfi) expansion. Babylon's token generation event (TGE) is imminent. ARB voted to invest 7,500 ETH in non-ARB protocols, while Montana rejected a Bitcoin reserve proposal. El Salvador is poised to halt Bitcoin purchases, as a strategy is devised to acquire an additional $2 billion worth of BTC. Hong Kong Asia Holdings plans to increase its BTC holdings. Arkham's spot trading is now operational in several states, and Monad's testnet was launched with a capacity of 10,000 transactions per second.

news.headline: BTC hits 3-month low as alts plunge post-Trump rally, with ETF outflows. $1.3bn liquidated, US ETFs see 2nd largest outflows. ETH risks 'Death Cross', South Dakota rejects BTC. Ohio mulls tax exemption for crypto, SEC nods at ADA ETF. Saylor pushes SEC for regulation change. HBAR ETF filed, Citadel ventures into crypto. Metaplanet acquires $12.9m BTC. OKX fined $500m+, Infini bank hit by $50m exploit. Dubai embraces USDC and EURC as tokens.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) has dropped to a 3-month low amidst significant outflows from Exchange-Traded Funds (ETF). Most cryptocurrencies are currently lower than they were before the surge attributed to former President Trump. ETFs in the US focused on BTC have experienced the second largest outflows of the year. This crypto plummet has resulted in approximately $1.3 billion in liquidations. The Greed and Fear index has shifted back to 'Extreme Fear' as a result. Ethereum (ETH) is on the brink of forming a 'Death Cross' pattern. South Dakota, following Montana's lead, has also rejected establishing a BTC reserve. Ohio is contemplating a bill that would prevent crypto from being taxed at the state level. Bybit has repaid Bitget's loan of 40 thousand ETH. The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has recognized Grayscale's ETF for Cardano (ADA). Canary Capital has filed for an ETF focused on Hedera Hashgraph (HBAR). MicroStrategy's Michael Saylor has advocated for regulatory reform to a task force within the SEC. The premium in strategy compared to BTC holdings has decreased to just 1.6 times. ETH's Pectra project is facing issues on its testnet with blocks going missing. FTX's CEO Sam Bankman-Fried tweeted from prison and praised Elon Musk's support for Dogecoin (DOGE). The SEC has concluded its investigation into Robinhood's crypto activities. Citadel is planning to enter the crypto trading space. Binance's CEO Changpeng Zhao has disclosed details about his private investment portfolio. Metaplanet has acquired an additional $12.9 million worth of BTC. OKX has been fined over $500 million for unlawfully serving customers in the US. Infini, a crypto neo-bank, has suffered a $50 million exploit. South Korea has imposed sanctions on the cryptocurrency exchange Upbit. Dubai has formally recognized USDC and EURC under its token regulations.

news.headline: EP14 of UNDER EXPOSED discusses Crypto Market Turmoil: Black Tuesday. Tune in every Tuesday from 12-1 PM EST on YouTube/X.
news.summary: Tune in to UNDER EXPOSED for in-depth coverage of the most significant macro news of the week affecting the crypto and broader markets. Hosted by TylerD, Deeze, Geebz, and Peter Jennings, the show delves into the latest trends, trades, and narratives in the industry. Get insights on the hottest sectors in crypto and NFTs, market movers, and the hosts' current investment strategies. Catch the live stream every Tuesday from 12:00 PM EST to 1:00 PM EST on YouTube and X. Don't miss out on the latest updates with the experts in the field.

news.headline: Bitcoin rebounds from $86k, ETF outflows hit record high due to basis unwinds. Fear & Greed at lowest since October. BTC predicted at $124k in 2025. SEC drops Uniswap case. MKR leads DeFi after Uniswap incident. IP surges 160% this week. Crypto in tactical retreat, not reversal. GME mulls $5bn BTC buy. Mirana Ventures transfers $600m ETH. Aya Miyaguchi named ETH foundation president. New SMS scam targets Binance. Bybit hackers launder $113m in 24hrs. Circle CEO advocates US registration of stablecoins. Tether CEO claims competitors aim to eliminate Tether.
news.summary: Bitcoin's price has bounced back from $86,000, with record-high outflows from Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs). The outflows are primarily driven by basis unwinds. The Fear & Greed Index has dropped to its lowest level since October. Prediction markets are only forecasting a $124,000 Bitcoin price by 2025. The current market strategy is down 55% from its peak. The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has dismissed the case against Uniswap. Maker (MKR) leads decentralized finance (DeFi) coins following the Uniswap news. Uniswap is being criticized as the "biggest disaster" in Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) governance. Internet Computer (IP) continues to outperform altcoins, rising 160% this week. HYPE has generated $3.5 million in fees in a single day. The crypto market is currently in a tactical retreat, not a full reversal, according to Teng. A Bitcoin bill in Oklahoma is moving to a floor vote. GameStop (GME) has received an investor proposal to buy $5 billion worth of Bitcoin. Mirana Ventures has transferred $600 million in Ethereum (ETH) to Bybit. Aya Miyaguchi has been appointed as the Ethereum Foundation president. The SEC has recognized the revised Grayscale Ethereum ETF. Solana (SOL) has submitted a proposal to reduce its issuance. A whale has moved $198 million worth of SOL to exchanges. A new SMS scam is targeting Binance users. Hackers on Bybit have laundered $113 million in the past 24 hours. The CEO of Circle is advocating for the US regulation of stablecoins. Tether's CEO has claimed that competitors are attempting to undermine Tether.

news.headline: Crypto culture in Consensus 2025: Rug Radio hosts Farokh with WalletConnect, Sophon, and MOG for insights on onboarding.
news.summary: During a recent interview on Rug Radio, Farokh Sarmad discussed the evolving crypto culture with Pedro Gomes, the Founder and Director of WalletConnect Foundation, Sebastien, the Founder and CEO of Sophon, and Mike Three, the Creative Director of MOG. The conversation delved into strategies for introducing newcomers to the crypto space.

news.headline: DASTAN CEO Fernandes & President Sarmad at Consensus 2025 in Hong Kong discuss MYRIAD, offering new media ecosystem solution.
news.summary: During Consensus 2025 held in Hong Kong, Loxley Fernandes, CEO of DASTAN, and Farokh Sarmad, President of the company, delve into the innovative features of MYRIAD, an on-chain prediction market. They highlighted how its dynamic participatory model presents a novel solution for encouraging cooperation within the media landscape.

news.headline: BTC hits $78K, blames tariffs for dip. Crypto market cap falls below 2021 peak. BTC ETFs lose $2.4bn this week.ETH down by 9% since election. Texas approves BTC reserve bill unanimously. Arizona moves closer to crypto law. BTC predicted to reach $500k pre-Trump. SEC withdraws Metamask case. ETH Foundation hires advisors. Stablecoin laws priority for Lummis. Metamask to include SOL, BTC support. BitMEX up for sale. Uniswap launches offramp. Coinbase, NEAR join for OpenAI. Base upgrades speed by 10x. ETH's update on track after test fail. SEC drops Consensys case.
news.summary: Bitcoin has surged to $78,000, nearly wiping out post-election gains, as the overall cryptocurrency market cap drops below its 2021 peak. Ethereum has seen a 9% decline since the election. Bitcoin ETFs have experienced outflows of $2.4 billion this week. Despite this, Bitcoin rebounded following a benign PCE inflation figure. Michael Saylor advises investing in Bitcoin even if it means extreme measures. Texas has passed a BTC reserve bill with a unanimous 9-0 vote, while Arizona's crypto bills are moving closer to becoming law. Analysts predict Bitcoin could reach $500,000 before Trump's term ends. The SEC has dropped a case against Metamask but remains silent on XRP. The SEC is also expected to drop an enforcement case against Consensys. The Ethereum Foundation has enlisted external advisors to uphold its values. Senator Lummis prioritizes stablecoin legislation. MetaMask is set to support SOL and BTC. Base has integrated Flashblocks for faster transactions. Ethereum's upgrade remains on schedule despite a recent test failure. BitMEX is reportedly up for sale as Uniswap partners with Moonpay for an offramp service. Coinbase and NEAR collaborate to develop OpenAI services. BitMEX seeks a buyer for its crypto exchange platform.

news.headline: Trump designates BTC, ETH, SOL, ADA, XRP for reserves. ADA surges 50%, XRP briefly outperforms ETH. BTC rises to 62%, ETH lags. $550m lost in liquidations. China, US planning reserves. BTC top pick for reserves. SOL climbs 20% post FTX unlock. Largest CME gap in BTC. $12m made on $200m bet. BTC ETF sees first outflow end. BlackRock adds BTC ETF. ETH ETF delay by SEC.
news.summary: Former President Trump has announced that Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), Cardano (ADA), and Ripple (XRP) have been selected for a reserve. The "Trump Put" phenomenon has sparked a rally in the market, particularly leading to a 50% surge in ADA's value. XRP briefly managed to surpass Ethereum's Fully Diluted Valuation (FDV). Despite underperforming, BTC bounced back to a 62% rise. Bears faced a significant setback, losing $550 million in Sunday liquidations. China is also considering establishing a strategic reserve, according to Bailey. Armstrong believes BTC is the top choice for a cryptocurrency reserve, while Hayes dismisses the idea as mere words without substance. Trump is expected to make an investment announcement today. However, the President of the Swiss National Bank (SNB) disagrees, stating that BTC is not a suitable reserve asset. SOL saw a 20% surge following a major FTX unlock. The largest CME gap to date has been recorded in BTC futures. A trader achieved a $12 million profit from a $200 million perpetual bet. The BTC ETF experienced an end to its 8-day outflow streak. BlackRock has included the BTC ETF in its model portfolios. Another delay by the SEC has been reported regarding ETH ETF options on CBOE.

news.headline: Crypto faces second-worst day with tariffs hitting markets. Reserve coins plunge by 20%+. Fear index at 24. BTC may reach $200T. SEC drops Kraken case. Coinbase questions SEC's crypto crackdown. DOGE ETF proposed by NYSE Arca. Tether appoints CFO for transparency. Vietnam to unveil crypto rules soon.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets saw a sudden reversal leading to a crash, marking it as the second-worst day for crypto. 'Reserve coins' collectively dropped by more than 20%. The Fear & Green index plummeted back to 24. Prominent figures in the crypto community criticized the inclusion of XRP and ADA in reserves. There is confusion surrounding the reasoning behind adding ETH and SOL to reserves according to Bernstein. The ETH/BTC ratio hit a 4-year low. Crypto Exchange-Traded Products (ETPs) experienced a significant outflow of $2.9 billion over a 3-week period. Reports emerged that $1.4 billion in Bybit funds were laundered through Thorchain. Speculation arose regarding potential tax breaks for cryptocurrency set to be announced on Friday. Michael Saylor predicted BTC will reach $200 trillion. An 'insider' at Hyperliquid gained $81 million from shorting ETH. The SEC revealed members of its crypto task force and announced plans to drop the case against Kraken. Coinbase launched an investigation into the costs associated with the SEC's 'war on crypto'. NYSE Arca proposed a rule change to list a Dogecoin ETF. Tether appointed a new CFO to enhance transparency. SBI in Japan pledged support for USDC while Australia disclosed no intentions of creating a crypto strategic reserve. Vietnam is set to introduce crypto regulations in March.

news.headline: EP15 of UNDER EXPOSED covers top macro news influencing crypto markets. Tune in Tue 12-1 PM EST on YouTube/X for insights on trends, trades, and more.
news.summary: Join UNDER EXPOSED for in-depth coverage of the most significant macro news of the week affecting the crypto and broader markets. Hosted by TylerD, Deeze, Geebz, and Peter Jennings, the show delves into the latest trends, trades, and narratives. Tune in every Tuesday from 12:00 PM EST to 1:00 PM EST for detailed discussions on the hottest sectors in crypto and NFTs, market movements, and investment strategies. The show streams live on YouTube and X, providing valuable insights for viewers.

news.headline: BTC surpasses 90k, new reserve policy on Friday. SOL reduces inflation by 80%. Metaplanet buys 43m BTC. Salinas holds 70% wealth in BTC. El Salvador continues buying BTC, Texas Senate to vote on BTC reserve. ETH activates Pectra update. Reddit Co-founder eyes TikTok on-chain. US brokers may repeal DeFi rule. USDT storage, MiCA regulations update. Crypto leaders to attend upcoming conference. Across Protocol secures 41m, AAVE leads alts with buyback news.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) has bounced back above $90,000 with its dominance increasing. An announcement on a new BTC reserve policy is expected on Friday, separate from how altcoins will be managed in reserves. Solana (SOL) has put forth a proposal to reduce inflation by 80%. Numerous crypto industry leaders are slated to participate in a conference scheduled for Friday. The US is gearing up to repeal the broker rule for decentralized finance (DeFi). Metaplanet has invested an additional $43 million in BTC. Billionaire Salinas has allocated 70% of his wealth to Bitcoin. The Texas Senate is preparing to vote on Bitcoin reserves. El Salvador has chosen to go against the International Monetary Fund (IMF) by continuing to purchase BTC. Storage and transfer of USD Tether (USDT) are now permitted under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation in the US. Ethereum (ETH) has implemented the Pectra update. The co-founder of Reddit is looking to bring TikTok onto the blockchain. Across Protocol successfully raised $41 million. AAVE has taken the lead among alternative cryptocurrencies after announcing a buyback plan.

news.headline: BTC positive on tariff delay, BioNexus starts ETH reserve. Coinbase to offer tokenised securities, NH BTC bill passes. Mt. Gox moves $1bn BTC. BioNexus pioneers ETH treasury. NY seeks to criminalize rug pulls. XRP donates $50m. ONDO surges after CEO invite. US customs to release seized miners. Jump to resume trading. Canary files for AXL ETF. Bitwise applies for Aptos ETF. Scale AI partners with Pentagon. AAVE to launch GHO savings coin. Stablecoins to surpass $1T. S. Korea nears BTC ETF call, Meliuz plans BTC treasury. Belarus weighs crypto mining, Argentina freezes LIBRA assets.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) experienced a surge in value due to the delay of tariffs and optimism from a conference, with sentiment still leaning towards 'Extreme Fear'. Coinbase is looking into offering tokenised securities, while a bill concerning BTC in New Hampshire successfully passed through the House committee. The USD's decline is seen as a positive indicator for a strong second quarter in the crypto market, as Mt. Gox shifted $1 billion worth of BTC to an unmarked address. BioNexus becomes the first public company to have an Ethereum (ETH) treasury. In New York, a bill is being introduced to make rug pulls illegal, as XRP donates $50 million to boost crypto awareness. ONDO saw a rise after its CEO received an invitation to a conference. U.S. customs is set to release seized crypto miners, and a platform named Jump is set to restart crypto trading. Canary has filed for the first AXL ETF, and Bitwise is looking to launch the Aptos ETF in the U.S. Scale AI has partnered with the Pentagon, and AAVE is moving towards a GHO savings coin. It is predicted that stablecoins will surpass $1 trillion by the end of the year according to Tomaino. South Korea is nearing a decision on a Bitcoin ETF, while Brazil's Meliuz plans to have a BTC treasury. Belarus is assessing the potential of crypto mining, and Argentina is taking action to freeze assets in a LIBRA fraud case.

news.headline: BTC dips after reserve plan; Nasdaq in correction. Treasury aims for neutral BTC accumulation. Early selling cost US $17bn. White House hosts crypto summit. TX senate passes BTC reserve bill. ETH sentiment at yearly low. Coinbase acquires Iron Fish team for privacy. SBF on Tucker Carlson’s podcast. DeFi interest spikes; Crypto VC hits $1.1b deals in Feb.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) experienced a decline in value as the treasury announced plans to fund its reserve through forfeitures, aiming for fiscal-neutral accumulation. A report revealed that the early selling of BTC resulted in a loss of $17 billion for the United States. A crypto summit is scheduled at the White House, with Senator Lummis potentially introducing a BTC proposal next week. The Texas Senate passed a bill regarding a BTC reserve. Analyst Klippsten believes there is a more than 50% chance of BTC reaching a new all-time high by June. A new bill has been proposed to stop the debanking of cryptocurrencies. The sentiment around Ethereum (ETH) reached a yearly low. Coinbase acquired the Iron Fish team to enhance privacy features. SBF made an appearance on Tucker Carlson's podcast. The price of SUI soared after a partnership with Trump's WLF. Canary has applied for a SUI ETF. The ByBit hack was linked to a compromise of a developer's laptop. Crypto venture capital deals amounted to $1.1 billion in February, with a surge in interest in decentralized finance (DeFi).

news.headline: BTC hits $80K and rebounds. Banks to enter crypto. $21B more BTC sought. Stablecoin legislation by August. Utah passes watered down BTC bill. US considering selling gold for BTC reserve. Fold Holdings acquires 475 BTC. US Dept. of Housing mulls using crypto. US finance media sees $110M crypto losses. Kraken eyes IPO by 1Q 2026. BBVA to offer BTC and ETH services. Coinbase plans to hire thousands.
news.summary: Bitcoin reached a high of $80,000 before experiencing a rebound in the market. The Feed & Greed index indicated 'Extreme fear' with a score of 20. The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency has granted banks broad access to the cryptocurrency market. A strategy was filed for a $21 billion perpetual stock issuance. Former President Trump announced plans for stablecoin legislation by August. The Utah Senate passed a diluted Bitcoin bill. There is speculation that the US may trade gold reserves for Bitcoin, according to StanChart. Not meeting expectations, Sacks is cautious about selling gold. Fold Holdings acquired an additional 475 Bitcoins. The US Department of Housing is considering adopting cryptocurrencies. WLFI reported losses of $110 million in the crypto market. Following an interview, SBF was moved to solitary confinement. Exchange platform Kraken is looking to go public in the first quarter of 2026. BBVA will soon provide Bitcoin and Ethereum services. Coinbase is planning to hire thousands in the US. Degen Spartan encourages perseverance in the market.

news.headline: BTC bounces back from $76k low after $1.1bn liquidations. ETH hits 16-month low. Stablecoin market cap ATH. Trump to end Operation Checkpoint. SEC eases crypto rules. Mt Gox $900m move. Senate to vote on stablecoin bill. Coinbase launches 24/7 futures. Deutsche Borse offers custody for BTC, ETH. Thai SEC approves USDT. Next coins to pump will be unique.
news.summary: Bitcoin experienced a significant drop, reaching a 4-month low of $76,000 before seeing a rebound. This downturn led to approximately $1.1 billion in liquidations and was accompanied by numerous large wipeouts. The Fear & Greed index fell to 14, its lowest level since 2022. Ethereum also hit a 16-month low, showing continued underperformance. A notable ETH liquidation event occurred at $1,127. Stablecoin market cap reached a new all-time high. Investors are foreseeing the impact of stimulus measures, as highlighted by Cathie Wood. Former President Trump is expected to sign an Executive Order to terminate Operation Checkpoint 2.0. The SEC is considering easing registration rules for crypto firms, while a Senate committee will vote on a stablecoin bill. Mt. Gox made a second movement of over $900 million within a week. Coinbase is set to introduce 24/7 perpetual-style futures trading in the US and has obtained a license in India. Deutsche Borse will provide custody services for BTC and ETH. An exchange in SGP launches open-ended BTC futures. ETH's Pectra upgrade faced disruptions. Lazarus group reportedly laundered $300 million from the ByBit hack. The Thai SEC has approved USDT. Offchain labs will include ARB tokens in its treasury. The next coins expected to surge will be different from the current trends.

news.headline: Oculis in Switzerland reports on equity awards to execs and shares purchase by board member on March 14, 2025.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in Zug, Switzerland, notifications were released regarding the annual equity incentive awards given to executive committee members of a company. Also, a member of the company's Board of Directors purchased ordinary shares. This demonstrates the company's commitment to rewarding its executives and directors.

news.headline: Caleres declares $0.07 per share quarterly dividend, payable on April 11, to shareholders as of March 27, 2025.
news.summary: Caleres, a prominent group of consumer-focused footwear brands, revealed its declaration of a quarterly cash dividend of $0.07 per share. This dividend will be paid out on April 11, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of March 27, 2025. Notably, the company has been consistently paying quarterly dividends for over a century. Caleres stands as a top player in the market, encompassing renowned footwear labels such as Famous Footwear and Allen Edmon.

news.headline: Excelerate Energy and PV Gas partner for US LNG supply by 2026. Collaboration for secure and stable supply.
news.summary: In The Woodlands, Texas, Excelerate Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EE), a prominent provider of floating storage and regasification units (FSRUs) and liquefied natural gas (LNG) solutions, has partnered with Petrovietnam Gas Joint Stock Corporation (PV Gas), a subsidiary of Vietnam Oil and Gas Group. They have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to work together on ensuring a consistent LNG supply from the United States starting in 2026. This collaboration aims to secure a reliable and stable source of LNG for both companies.

news.headline: Hennessy Advisors to acquire STF Management ETFs, assets of two ETFs purchased, welcomes portfolio manager Jonathan Molchan.
news.summary: Hennessy Advisors, Inc. based in Novato, California, disclosed on March 14, 2025, that they have reached a definitive agreement with STF Management, LP ("STFM") to broaden their ETF offerings. This development includes acquiring the assets of two ETFs as part of their strategy to enhance their investment options. The company, listed on Nasdaq as HNNA, aims to expand its market presence through this agreement.

news.headline: Weatherford to hold conference call on April 23, 2025, to discuss first quarter results ending March 31, 2025.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, Weatherford International plc announced plans to hold a conference call on April 23, 2025, to review its performance in the first quarter of the year, which concluded on March 31, 2025. The conference call will focus on discussing the results of the Company during this period. Weatherford International plc is traded publicly on the NASDAQ under the symbol WFRD.

news.headline: Stoneridge, Inc. (NYSE: SRI) in Novi, Mich. reported board changes as George S. Mayes, Jr. and Paul J. Schlather retire.
news.summary: Novi, Michigan - On March 14, 2025, Stoneridge, Inc. revealed updates regarding its Board of Directors. After a long tenure, George S. Mayes, Jr. and Paul J. Schlather will be stepping down from the Board due to personal reasons. They have decided against seeking reelection. Stoneridge, Inc. is listed on the NYSE under the symbol SRI. This change highlights the departure of key board members, signaling a shift in leadership within the company.

news.headline: Trinity Biotech fails Nasdaq rules with under $1.00 per share bid price and under $15M market value of publicly held shares.
news.summary: Dublin-based biotechnology company, Trinity Biotech plc, trading as Nasdaq: TRIB, specializes in human diagnostics and diabetes management solutions, including wearable biosensors. Recently, the company was notified by Nasdaq that it does not meet the requirements set by Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450(a)(1), which mandates a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share, based on the closing bid price of the Company’s American depositary shares for the past 30 consecutive business days. Additionally, it was noted that Trinity Biotech plc does not meet the criteria outlined in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450(b) that mandates a minimum market value of publicly held shares of $15,000,000, according to Nasdaq’s evaluation of the company's market value for the last 30 consecutive business days.

news.headline: Nokia Corp buys back shares on 14.03.2025.
Arch Biopartners closed a non-brokered private placement with 145,000 CAD $1.55 per share and 90,000 USD $1.15 per share totaling CAD $374,000.
news.summary: In Toronto on March 14, 2025, Arch Biopartners Inc. (referred to as “Arch” or the “Company”), confirmed the completion of a non-brokered private placement transaction. The offering consisted of 145,000 common shares valued at CAD $1.55 per share and 90,000 common shares valued at USD $1.15 each (collectively termed as the “Common Shares”). The total sum gathered from this Offering was CAD $374,000. Arch's shares are listed on the TSX Venture exchange under the ticker symbol ARCH and on the OTCQB under ACHFF.

news.headline: US investigation into crucial shipping points signals wider international influence.
Beyond Oil receives payment for 21.6T order from Latitude under a 5-year agreement with minimum commitments of US$8.3M for 2025.
news.summary: Beyond Oil has confirmed that it received full payment for Latitude's March order, with delivery currently underway. This recent order represents a significant uptick, surpassing February's figures by over 30% and January's by 100%. The transaction is part of a long-term partnership, exemplified by a five-year agreement requiring minimum commitments amounting to US$8.3 million for 2025 alone.

news.headline: UK authorizes charges against NCA officer for alleged $75,000 Bitcoin theft, due to face 15 related offenses in court. Bull run increased value to $4.2M. Changes in law allow seizure of crypto without arrest. NCA seized $26M in cash and crypto to combat money laundering in global campaign. Regulatory framework for digital assets expected in the UK following election victory.
news.summary: A National Crime Agency officer in England and Wales is facing charges for allegedly stealing 50 Bitcoin worth about $75,000 in 2017. The officer, Paul Chowles, is charged with 15 offenses including theft, concealing criminal property, and using criminal property. The Bitcoin is now valued at over $4.2 million at a price of $84,541 per BTC. Chowles is set to appear in court on April 25. Recent amendments allow UK authorities to seize crypto from suspected criminals without arrest. In December 2024, the NCA seized $26 million in cash and crypto during a global anti-money laundering operation. The UK government is expected to establish a regulatory framework for digital assets in 2025, making it a significant market for cryptocurrencies.

news.headline: Trump: Tariffs will remain in place until drug flow stops.
Nvidia's high stock valuations bring concerns of potential decline.
Financier of Pirate Bay, Carl Lundstrom, dies in plane crash. Convicted in 2009 for aiding copyright infringement. Supporter of far-right causes.
news.summary: Lundstrom, a former far-right supporter and unsuccessful political candidate, was convicted in 2009 for assisting in copyright infringement.

news.headline: Former Raytheon CEO, Bill Swanson, transitions to wind energy sector after a successful career in weapons industry.
US Senate Minority Leader Schumer: The Senate Democrats are presented with two bad choices.
Details on the operations of the US Bitcoin Reserve remain unclear; key information is yet to be disclosed.
US Senate Minority Leader Schumer: The stopgap bill is bad, but a shutdown is worse.
US Senate Minority Leader Schumer: The Senate will vote on a bill with a DC funding fix.
Liberty files final prospectus, official announcement made.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.5%. This significant drop in unemployment is attributed to the addition of 250,000 new jobs in the past month alone. Furthermore, the report highlighted a steady growth in wages, with an average increase of 3.2% over the last year. The strong job market has also led to an increase in consumer spending, which has had a positive impact on the overall economy. Experts predict that this trend is likely to continue in the upcoming months, indicating a stable and prosperous economic outlook for the country.

news.headline: Blackstone Mortgage Trust (NYSE: BXMT) declares $0.47 per share dividend for Q1 2025, payable on April 15 to stockholders of record by March 31.
news.summary: In New York, Blackstone Mortgage Trust, Inc. (NYSE: BXMT) has announced a dividend of $0.47 per share for class A common stock for the first quarter of 2025. Shareholders of record by March 31, 2025, will receive the dividend on April 15, 2025. Blackstone Mortgage Trust, a real estate finance company (NYSE: BXMT), focuses on originating senior loans backed by commercial real estate in North America and Europe.

news.headline: Kinetik's subsidiary prices $250M private placement of 6.625% sustainability-linked senior notes due 2028 at 101.25% of par.
news.summary: Kinetik Holdings Inc. (NYSE: KNTK) from Houston and Midland, Texas, shared that its subsidiary, Kinetik Holdings LP, has priced the offering of $250 million 6.625% sustainability-linked senior notes due 2028 at 101.25% of par, along with accrued and unpaid interest from December 15, 2024. These New Notes mature on December 15, 2028, with an annual interest rate of 6.625% and payment scheduled on June 15.

news.headline: World Kinect Corp declares $0.17 quarterly cash dividend per share, payable on April 16, 2025 to shareholders of record on March 31, 2025 (NYSE: WKC).
news.summary: In Miami, World Kinect Corporation (NYSE: WKC) disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.17 per share. This dividend will be disbursed on April 16, 2025, to shareholders on record as of March 31, 2025. World Kinect Corporation, based in Miami, Florida, is a worldwide energy management provider catering to over 150,000 clients in the aviation, marine, and land-based sectors.

news.headline: indie Semiconductor grants equity awards to new employees under 2023 Inducement Incentive Plan approved by Board. Website has details.
news.summary: Aliso Viejo, California-based indie Semiconductor (Nasdaq: INDI), a key player in automotive solutions, disclosed the issuance of equity awards known as the “Inducement Grants” from its 2023 Inducement Incentive Plan to fresh hires. The allotment, sanctioned by indie Semiconductor's Board of Directors’ Compensation Committee, is now accessible on the firm's investor relations webpage: https://investors.ind.

news.headline: Champion Homes releases EcoWise™ homes in Troy, MI, aiming to offset annual energy use with renewable energy systems.
news.summary: Champion Homes, Inc. introduces EcoWise™, a new initiative focused on sustainability at their Troy location. The program involves incorporating high-performance enhancements into manufactured homes to enhance energy efficiency. These upgrades are so effective that a renewable energy system could potentially offset the entire annual energy consumption of the home. Notably, the EcoWise™ initiative showcases Champion Homes's most energy-efficient designs yet. These homes not only comply with but also surpass the federal government's stringent standards for high-performance residences. This commitment to sustainability highlights Champion Homes's dedication to reducing energy consumption.

news.headline: US VP Vance: I expects a high-level TikTok deal by the April 5th deadline - NBC News.
Blackstone Credit & Insurance Closed-End Funds announce monthly distributions on March 14, 2025. #Blackstone #Credit #Insurance
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, Blackstone Liquid Credit Strategies LLC, a subsidiary of Blackstone Alternative Credit Advisors LP, revealed plans in collaboration with other Blackstone credit-focused affiliates under Blackstone, Inc., collectively known as "Blackstone Credit & Insurance." This partnership aims to make strides in the credit sector.

news.headline: Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. appoints Ronald Ballschmiede as interim CFO, Sharon Villaverde departs.
news.summary: In a recent press release from The Woodlands, Texas on March 14, 2025, Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. (NasdaqGS: STRL) revealed that Ronald A. Ballschmiede, the Executive Vice President of the company, has been appointed as the interim Principal Financial Officer. This news signifies a shift in leadership roles within the company, with Ballschmiede taking on additional responsibilities. Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. continues to make strategic decisions to navigate the financial landscape, as indicated by this appointment. The NasdaqGS: STRL stockholders and stakeholders will likely observe the impact of this change in the company's financial operations moving forward.

news.headline: M/I Homes announces retirement of Friedrich K.M. Böhm, a board member since 1994. Important news for shareholders.
news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio on March 14, 2025, M/I Homes, Inc. (NYSE:MHO) shared news that Friedrich K.M. Böhm, a longstanding independent member of the Company's Board of Directors since 1994, has chosen to retire from the Board once his term concludes.

news.headline: GBank Financial Holdings Inc. has filed Amendment No. 1 to Form S-1 Registration Statement. (Characters: 87)
news.summary: In Las Vegas on March 14, 2025, GBank Financial Holdings Inc. revealed that it, as the parent company of GBank, submitted Amendment No. 1 to Form S-1 Registration Statement under the Securities Act of 1933. The Company is listed as OTCQX: GBFH. This update signifies a significant move in the financial sector and showcases the company's commitment to transparency and regulatory compliance. The details of this amendment and its implications on the Company's operations and future prospects are of interest to stakeholders and investors, as it could potentially impact the financial market and the bank's performance. Stay tuned for further developments on this front.

news.headline: Globus Maritime files 2024 Annual Report on Form 20-F with audited financial statements for year ended Dec 31, 2024.
news.summary: In Glyfada, Greece, on March 14, 2025, Globus Maritime Limited, a dry bulk shipping company listed on NASDAQ under GLBS, disclosed that their annual report (Form 20-F) featuring audited financial statements for the fiscal year closing on December 31, 2024, has been submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission. The report is now accessible on Globus Maritime Limited's official website at www.globusmaritime.gr within the Investor Relations section.
